title: Rytigynia neglecta
taxonomy: Rytigynia neglecta is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the madder family. The genus Rytigynia is a part of the tribe Vanguerieae, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. Rytigynia neglecta is a plant species endemic to Tanzania, and its classification is primarily based on botanical characteristics such as leaf shape and flower structure. The plant is closely related to other species within the Rytigynia genus, which share similar morphological features. Further research may be needed to confirm the precise classification of Rytigynia neglecta within the Rubiaceae family.
